req.title: System-Admin updates expense-claim
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a expense-claim through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path update by an authenticated admin yielding 200/201 plus an audit record; (2) validation failure (missing mandatory field) yielding 400; (3) unauthenticated access yielding 401; (4) forbidden role yielding 403; (5) boundary conditions on a string field (e.g. description at max allowed length). EP partitions the valid/invalid input space; BVA targets the max-length boundary of a mandatory string field; STC models the expense-claim state machine transition from 'submitted' to 'approved'; DT covers the combination of auth × role × payload validity; NEG exercises each error pathway independently.

tests.name: test_admin_update_expense_claim_success
preconditions: A valid expense-claim record exists with known id
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <get_admin_token>
Create or retrieve an existing expense-claim id via <create_expense_claim>
Send PUT /api/expense-claims/{id} with all mandatory fields populated
Inspect response status and body
Query audit log via <get_audit_records> for the expense-claim id
expected: Response body contains the expense-claim entity id
Audit log contains at least one record for this expense-claim id
trace: R-WEB-042: authenticated admin can update expense-claim
R-WEB-042: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-042: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_admin_update_expense_claim_success(base_url, get_admin_token, create_expense_claim, get_audit_records):
    token = get_admin_token()
    claim_id = create_expense_claim(token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "description": "Updated office supplies",
        "amount": 150.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "category": "office",
        "status": "approved"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(claim_id) in str(body.get("id", "")) or body.get("id") == claim_id
    audit_entries = get_audit_records(claim_id, token)
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "No audit record written for the update"

tests.name: test_update_expense_claim_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: A valid expense-claim record exists with known id
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <get_admin_token>
Create or retrieve an existing expense-claim id via <create_expense_claim>
Send PUT /api/expense-claims/{id} with mandatory field 'amount' omitted
Inspect response status and body
expected: Response body contains a validation error message referencing the missing field
trace: R-WEB-042: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_update_expense_claim_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(base_url, get_admin_token, create_expense_claim):
    token = get_admin_token()
    claim_id = create_expense_claim(token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "description": "Missing amount field",
        "currency": "USD",
        "category": "office"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "amount" in error_text or "validation" in error_text or "required" in error_text

tests.name: test_update_expense_claim_description_at_max_length_boundary
preconditions: Maximum allowed length for description field is known (255 characters)
A valid expense-claim record exists with known id
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <get_admin_token>
Create or retrieve an existing expense-claim id via <create_expense_claim>
Build a description string of exactly 255 characters
Send PUT /api/expense-claims/{id} with description at max boundary
Inspect response status and body
expected: Response body contains the expense-claim entity id
trace: R-WEB-042: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-042: response 200 or 201 with entity id
BVA: upper boundary of description field length
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_update_expense_claim_description_at_max_length_boundary(base_url, get_admin_token, create_expense_claim):
    token = get_admin_token()
    claim_id = create_expense_claim(token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    max_description = "A" * 255
    payload = {
        "description": max_description,
        "amount": 99.99,
        "currency": "USD",
        "category": "travel",
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 at max boundary, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("description") == max_description, "Description not persisted at max boundary length"

tests.name: test_update_expense_claim_unauthenticated_returns_401
preconditions: A valid expense-claim id is known
actions: Send PUT /api/expense-claims/{id} with a valid payload but without Authorization header
Inspect response status
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_update_expense_claim_unauthenticated_returns_401(base_url, known_claim_id):
    payload = {
        "description": "No auth attempt",
        "amount": 50.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "category": "office",
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/expense-claims/{known_claim_id}",
        json=payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_update_expense_claim_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but holds role 'employee' (not admin)
A valid expense-claim id is known
actions: Obtain non-admin auth token via <get_employee_token>
Send PUT /api/expense-claims/{id} with a valid payload using employee token
Inspect response status
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_update_expense_claim_forbidden_role_returns_403(base_url, get_employee_token, known_claim_id):
    token = get_employee_token()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "description": "Employee attempting update",
        "amount": 75.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "category": "office",
        "status": "submitted"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/expense-claims/{known_claim_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_expense_claim_state_transition_submitted_to_approved
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <get_admin_token>
Create expense-claim in 'submitted' state via <create_expense_claim_in_state>
Send PUT /api/expense-claims/{id} with status changed to 'approved'
Inspect response status and returned status field
Query audit log via <get_audit_records> for state-change entry
expected: Audit log records the state transition from 'submitted' to 'approved'
trace: R-WEB-042: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_expense_claim_state_transition_submitted_to_approved(base_url, get_admin_token, create_expense_claim_in_state, get_audit_records):
    token = get_admin_token()
    claim_id = create_expense_claim_in_state(token, status="submitted")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "description": "Approve after review",
        "amount": 200.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "category": "travel",
        "status": "approved"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/api/expense-claims/{claim_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "approved", f"Expected status 'approved', got {body.get('status')}"
    audit_entries = get_audit_records(claim_id, token)
    transitions = [e for e in audit_entries if "approved" in str(e).lower()]
    assert len(transitions) >= 1, "Audit log missing state-transition entry to 'approved'"
